sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Despite Billions in Federal Subsidies, Chipmakers Are Laying Off Thousands of Workers
feed.summary: Despite significant federal subsidies from the CHIPS Act, major semiconductor manufacturers like Micron Technology, Intel, AMD, Nvidia, and Qualcomm are implementing layoffs or hiring freezes due to slowing demand in key markets. Micron, one of the largest memory chip makers, plans to cut 10% of its workforce, impacting 4,800 employees, citing a "dramatic drop in demand" for PC and smartphone chips. While these companies invest heavily in new facilities to leverage tax breaks, government incentives are not alleviating their short-term financial challenges exacerbated by reduced consumer spending.

feed.title: The bright side of the chip glut: Nvidia, AMD and Intel gaming cards are cheap and very available for Christmas
feed.summary: The article highlights that due to a chip glut, gaming cards from Nvidia, AMD, and Intel are now readily available and significantly discounted, making them an affordable option for Christmas shoppers. This marks a significant change from the previous two years when these items were scarce and expensive. The availability addresses past disappointments for gamers hoping for these cards during the holidays.

feed.title: Micron Technology Sees Chip Recovery by the End of 2023
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. stock has fallen significantly in 2022 due to weak demand for DRAM chips and an oversupply in the market. Despite a challenging fiscal Q1 2023 with missed earnings and lowered guidance, Micron expects improving customer inventories to lead to increased revenue in the fiscal second half of 2023. Analysts have revised their ratings and price targets, with some maintaining overweight or buy ratings despite the current downturn.

feed.title: Applied Materials Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
feed.summary: Shares of Applied Materials Inc. dropped 1.17% to $94.23 on Wednesday, marking its fourth consecutive day of losses, despite outperforming the broader S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, although it performed better than several key competitors like Broadcom, Qualcomm, and Advanced Micro Devices. Trading volume was also below its 50-day average.

feed.title: TSMC starts mass-producing three-nanometer chips
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd. (TSMC) has begun mass-producing processors using its advanced three-nanometer chip technology. This new technology offers a 15% improvement in chip performance and up to 35% less power consumption compared to its earlier five-nanometer process. TSMC, a major contract chip manufacturer for companies like Apple, AMD, and Nvidia, plans to develop multiple versions of this technology and is building new fabrication plants in Arizona.

feed.title: Sales of computer graphics cards slump to lowest level in two decades
feed.summary: Sales of computer graphics cards hit a 20-year low in Q3 2022 due to a global economic slowdown and the impact of the Ethereum merge on cryptocurrency mining. Major chipmakers like AMD and Nvidia experienced significant declines in discrete desktop GPU shipments, with AMD particularly hard hit. Despite the slump, new high-end GPU launches and Intel's entry into the market (grabbing a 4% share) offer potential for future sales boosts.

feed.title: TSMC ramps up 3nm chip production in Taiwan
feed.summary: TSMC has officially begun mass production of 3nm chips at its facilities in southern Taiwan, a process expected to offer substantial improvements in density, performance, and power efficiency compared to its 5nm node. This move comes as TSMC balances global expansion, including new fabs in the US, with a continued commitment to developing its most advanced technology in Taiwan, navigating complex geopolitical trade relations.

feed.title: New AI/ML processor supports SoCs and chiplets for smart edge devices
feed.summary: CEVA, Inc. has introduced NeuPro-M, a new AI/ML processor architecture designed for inference workloads in smart edge devices. This heterogeneous architecture boasts significant performance improvements (5-15X) over its predecessor, supporting scalability up to 1,200 TOPS and offering robust security features. NeuPro-M is optimized for a wide range of neural networks, featuring advanced capabilities like a fully programmable vector processing unit and sophisticated memory management for enhanced power efficiency and utilization.

feed.title: Cloudflare didn’t want cybersecurity companies silencing speech. Anti-trans trolls took advantage of the generosity
feed.summary: Cloudflare initially defended its stance against deplatforming Kiwi Farms, a forum known for harassing marginalized communities, arguing against becoming an arbiter of online content. However, due to escalating threats targeting human life, Cloudflare reversed its decision and blocked Kiwi Farms, highlighting the difficult balance cybersecurity providers face between free speech principles and safety. This incident, following prior actions against 8chan and Daily Stormer, underscores the complex challenges internet infrastructure companies encounter in navigating content moderation without clear legislative guidelines.

feed.title: Astera Labs Enters Pre-Production Phase of Leo Memory Connectivity Platform for CXL-Attached Memory Expansion and Pooling
feed.summary: Astera Labs announced its Leo Memory Connectivity Platform, supporting CXL 1.1 and 2.0, has entered pre-production sampling for customers and partners. This platform aims to solve memory bottlenecks and enable secure, reliable, and high-performance memory expansion and pooling for cloud servers. The announcement highlights successful interoperability testing and addresses the growing demand for memory in AI and ML applications.

feed.title: NVIDIA Ada Lovelace Breaks Energy-Efficiency Barrier, Supercharges 170+ Laptop Designs for Gamers and Creators
feed.summary: NVIDIA has announced its new GeForce RTX 40 Series laptops, powered by the Ada Lovelace GPU architecture, which promise significant generational leaps in performance and power efficiency. These laptops, featuring DLSS 3 and fifth-generation Max-Q technologies, offer up to 4x performance for gamers and blazing-fast acceleration for creators, with models ranging from high-end flagships to more accessible options. The new technology also enables powerful 14-inch laptops and boosts the NVIDIA Studio ecosystem for over 110 million creators.

feed.title: GeForce RTX 40 Series Studio Laptops, Omniverse Updates Accelerate AI-Powered Content Creation
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced new GeForce RTX 40 Series Studio Laptops and updates to its Omniverse platform, designed to accelerate AI-powered content creation workflows. These developments, showcased at CES, include improvements to Studio software like Broadcast and Canvas, the introduction of RTX Video Super Resolution, and new GeForce RTX 4070 Ti graphics cards for desktops. The article highlights how these technologies empower artists with significant performance boosts in 3D, video, and AI-driven creative tasks, facilitating real-time collaboration and faster content delivery.

feed.title: The War Over the World’s Most Critical Technology: A Conversation with Chris Miller
feed.summary: This article features a conversation with Chris Miller, author of "Chip War," discussing the geopolitical importance of semiconductors, particularly in the U.S.-China rivalry. It explores Taiwan's critical role in advanced chip manufacturing through TSMC, the potential risks of a conflict over Taiwan, and the challenges faced by the U.S. and China in reducing their reliance on Taiwanese chip production.

feed.title: AMD Extends its Leadership with the Introduction of its Broadest Portfolio of High-Performance PC Products for Mobile and Desktop
feed.summary: AMD announced an expansion of its high-performance PC product portfolio for both mobile and desktop at CES 2023. This includes new Ryzen 7000X3D Series Desktop processors with 3D V-Cache technology for gamers and creators, and Ryzen 7000 Series Mobile processors, some featuring the debut of dedicated AI hardware with Ryzen AI technology. The company aims to offer enhanced performance and efficiency across its new offerings to cater to various user needs.

feed.title: AMD Highlights Future of High-Performance and Adaptive Computing During Opening Keynote of CES 2023
feed.summary: During the CES 2023 opening keynote, AMD Chair and CEO Dr. Lisa Su showcased new leadership products across various markets, emphasizing the role of high-performance and adaptive computing in solving global challenges. AMD unveiled new mobile and desktop processors, including the first x86 PC CPU with a dedicated AI engine and desktop processors with 3D V-Cache technology, alongside new graphics solutions. The company also previewed advancements in AI with the Alveo V70 AI Accelerator and Instinct MI300, and highlighted the use of AMD technology in healthcare and aerospace.

feed.title: AMD Unveils Suite of New Radeon GPUs to Power High-Performance, Power-Efficient Gaming Laptops
feed.summary: AMD announced its new Radeon RX 7000 Series Graphics for Laptops at CES 2023, built on the RDNA 3 architecture for exceptional energy efficiency and performance. This includes the Radeon RX 7000M Series for high performance-per-watt and the Radeon RX 7000S Series optimized for thin-and-light laptops. These new GPUs aim to deliver high-framerate gaming at ultra settings and support advanced features like dedicated AI and raytracing hardware, AV1 encoding, and AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution technology.

feed.title: HP at CES 2023: Leading in Hybrid and Gaming Experiences
feed.summary: At CES 2023, HP Inc. announced a new line-up of products and solutions aimed at enhancing hybrid work and gaming experiences, focusing on sustainability. Key introductions include the HP Dragonfly Pro for freelancers, the Poly Voyager Free 60 Series earbuds, new HP E-Series monitors, and OMEN Gaming Hub with NVIDIA GeForce NOW integration. HP also emphasized its commitment to sustainability by incorporating unique recycled materials like coffee grounds and ocean-bound plastics into its new PCs and peripherals.

feed.title: HP launches PCs for hybrid workers with 'world-first' dual-camera setups
feed.summary: HP unveiled new PCs at CES 2023, specifically designed for hybrid workers, featuring what it calls a 'world-first' dual-camera setup that allows for simultaneous video streams and camera switching. These devices, including the HP Dragonfly G4, HP EliteBook 1040 G10, and HP Elite x360 1040 G10, are equipped with Intel 13th Gen processors and aim to enhance productivity and connectivity in flexible work environments. Additionally, HP introduced the Dragonfly Pro Chromebook and HP Dragonfly Pro targeted at users needing continuous connectivity, powerful cameras, and dedicated support for demanding workloads, appealing to the growing freelance community.

feed.title: CES 2023: AMD seeks to attract Nvidia’s customers with advanced new AI chips
feed.summary: AMD announced its new Instinct MI300 chip at CES 2023, aiming to compete with Nvidia in the AI market. The chip combines a CPU and GPU to accelerate the intensive process of developing artificial intelligence models in data centers. AMD also highlighted new energy-efficient laptops capable of extended video playback.

feed.title: CES 2023: AMD, Nvidia, auto applications get the hype, but analysts say this one chip maker ruled
feed.summary: At CES 2023, while companies like Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Nvidia Corp. received significant attention, analysts suggest that a lower-profile chip maker is better positioned post-convention. Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore noted ongoing caution regarding overall chip demand, particularly due to softness in China, but highlighted autos as a strong theme at the event.

feed.title: Hypatia Women CEO ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:WCEO)
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the Hypatia Women CEO ETF (WCEO), including its current price, holdings, and news. The ETF, which launched on January 9, 2023, focuses on capital appreciation by investing in US companies led by female Chief Executive Officers. The report outlines key statistics, expense ratios, performance details, and lists its top ten holdings.

feed.title: Intel 4th Gen Xeon series offers a leap in data center performance and efficiency
feed.summary: Intel has launched its 4th Gen Intel Xeon scalable processors, including the Intel Xeon CPU Max Series and Intel Data Center GPU Max Series, featuring enhanced security and new AI capabilities. These processors promise significant improvements in data center performance and efficiency, offering workload-first acceleration and optimized software for better total cost of ownership. The new line boasts built-in accelerators, power savings, and expanded confidential computing features designed to address critical computing challenges and sustainability goals.

feed.title: Intel Launches 4th Gen Xeon Scalable Processors, Max Series CPUs and GPUs
feed.summary: Intel has launched its 4th Gen Xeon Scalable processors, along with the Intel Xeon CPU Max Series and Intel Data Center GPU Max Series. These new processors aim to deliver significant improvements in performance, efficiency, and capabilities for AI, cloud, network, edge, and high-performance computing, emphasizing built-in accelerators and sustainability features. The company highlights broad industry adoption and performance gains across various key workloads.

feed.title: Nvidia debuts new DGX H100 systems powered by Intel’s 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable chips
feed.summary: Nvidia has unveiled new DGX H100 systems that integrate its H100 Tensor Core GPUs with Intel's recently launched 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors. These systems, along with upcoming server offerings from Nvidia's partners, promise significantly enhanced efficiency and performance for AI workloads, boasting up to 25 times more efficiency and 3.5 times better AI training/inference efficiency compared to previous generation machines. The new hardware leverages PCIe Gen 5 for faster data transfer and includes a free license for Nvidia Enterprise AI software.

feed.title: AMD poaches Marvell CFO Jean Hu to replace its retiring longtime fiscal chief
feed.summary: Jean Hu, previously CFO at Marvell Technology, is moving to Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to take over as CFO. This transition follows the retirement of AMD's longtime fiscal chief, creating a significant change in leadership for both companies. The move highlights a key personnel change in the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: AMD Announces CFO Transition
feed.summary: AMD announced that its Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, Devinder Kumar, will retire, with Jean Hu set to join as the new Executive Vice President and CFO on January 23, 2023. Kumar will remain with the company through April 2023 to ensure a smooth transition. Jean Hu previously served as CFO at Marvell and brings over 20 years of financial leadership experience in the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: AMD, Marvell Technology Name New CFOs
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Marvell Technology have announced new Chief Financial Officers. Jean Hu, previously CFO at Marvell Technology, is moving to AMD for the same role. Willem Meintjes will take over as CFO at Marvell Technology, effective January 20th.

feed.title: AMD poaches Marvell’s Jean Hu for CFO slot as Devinder Kumar retires
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. announced that its financial chief Devinder Kumar is retiring, and Jean Hu from Marvell Technology Inc. will take his place as CFO. Marvell confirmed Hu's departure effective January 20, while AMD expects Hu to start on January 23. This change marks a significant leadership transition for AMD's financial department.

feed.title: March 3rd Options Now Available For Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)
feed.summary: New options contracts for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) with a March 3rd expiration date are now available. Investors can consider a put contract at a $68.00 strike price with a current bid of $4.50, potentially leading to a 6.62% return if it expires worthless. Alternatively, a covered call strategy using a $72.00 strike price contract with a current bid of $3.70 could yield a 10.32% return if the stock is called away.

feed.title: AMD is making history for Asian women executives
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is making history by appointing Jean Hu as CFO, resulting in an S&P 500 company led by women of Asian descent in the Chair, CEO, and CFO roles. This move is significant given the barriers Asian-American women face in reaching C-suite positions. Hu will transition into her role starting Jan. 23, succeeding Devinder Kumar.

feed.title: How Both Taiwan and the US Can Benefit from TSMC's Investment
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is increasing its investment in the U.S. to $40 billion, building a second factory in Phoenix, Arizona. This move, supported by the CHIPS and Science Act, aims to strengthen the U.S. domestic semiconductor industry and diversify global supply chains. Experts suggest that while complete self-sufficiency for the U.S. might be unrealistic, this investment helps address critical dependencies, enhances national security, and fosters innovation in chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Taiwan ASE plant listed as 'lighthouse factory' by WEF
feed.summary: Advanced Semiconductor Engineering Inc (ASE) has been recognized as a "lighthouse factory" by the World Economic Forum (WEF) for its Kaohsiung bumping factory. The factory was inducted into the WEF's Global Lighthouse Network due to its adoption of Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) technologies, specifically AI-enabled processes. These innovations led to a 67% increase in output and a 39% reduction in order lead time.

feed.title: Biometric Sensors Market Size to Touch USD 3.5829 million by 2030 According to Acumen Research and Consulting
feed.summary: The global biometric sensors market is projected to grow from USD 1.3195 million in 2021 to USD 3.5829 million by 2030, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.9% from 2022 to 2030. This growth is driven by increasing demand for secure authentication, advancements in technology, and wider adoption in various sectors like healthcare, banking, and government. Despite its growth, the market faces challenges due to lack of standardization, high implementation costs, and privacy concerns.

feed.title: Spot On: Texas Instruments Announces First Chipset That Enables Cameras and Sensors to Clean Themselves
feed.summary: Texas Instruments has introduced the first semiconductors with ultrasonic lens cleaning (ULC) technology, enabling camera systems to self-clean dirt, ice, and water using microscopic vibrations. This innovation addresses the problem of dirty camera lenses in various applications, including autonomous vehicles, by offering a cost-effective and compact solution that improves system accuracy and reduces maintenance. The chipset includes the ULC1001 digital signal processor and DRV2901 piezo transducer driver, featuring proprietary algorithms for automatic sensing and cleaning.

feed.title: New AMD Kria ODM Ecosystem Program Helps Developers Quickly Bring Adaptive Solutions to Market
feed.summary: AMD has launched the Kria ODM Partner Ecosystem, an initiative designed to help developers bring adaptive solutions to market faster using production-grade, Kria SOM-based platforms. This program offers turnkey solutions with full software stacks and application support from established partners like Ectron, Optomotive, and VVDN. These pre-built platforms, initially targeting sectors like smart cities and industrial IoT, leverage the adaptability of Kria SOMs and can be customized and branded for rapid deployment.

feed.title: T.J. Rodgers has brought in a pair of chip industry vets to right the ship at Enovix
feed.summary: T.J. Rodgers, executive chairman and major shareholder of Enovix, has brought in new leadership from the chip industry to turn around the struggling battery maker. Raj Talluri, formerly of Qualcomm, has been appointed CEO, tasked with instilling a "silicon mindset" following production setbacks. This move aims to stabilize Enovix's operations and strategy.

feed.title: AMD Adaptive Computing Technology Enables Next-Generation DENSO LiDAR System, Delivering 20X Improvement in Resolution
feed.summary: AMD announced that its adaptive computing technology, specifically the AMD Xilinx Automotive (XA) Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC, will power DENSO Corporation’s next-generation LiDAR platform. This new platform, set to ship in 2025, promises a 20X improvement in resolution and extremely low latency for enhanced precision in object detection, achieving ISO 26262 ASIL-B certification. The technology enables high point-cloud density in SPAD LiDAR systems, contributing to smaller form factors and allowing for multiple LiDAR implementations in next-generation autonomous vehicles and other applications.

feed.title: Add Exposure to Semiconductor Stocks with These 2 Invesco ETFs
feed.summary: As semiconductor stocks experience a surge, investors can gain exposure to the industry through two Invesco ETFs: the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) and the Invesco Dynamic Semiconductors ETF (PSI). Both ETFs were up over 4% on the day and approximately 16% year-to-date, driven by positive analyst sentiment and significant gains in companies like Nvidia and AMD. SOXQ and PSI offer exposure to 30 U.S. semiconductor companies, with PSI focusing on investment merit criteria and SOXQ tracking the performance of the largest U.S.-listed semiconductor firms.

feed.title: AMD can keep eating Intel’s lunch, Barclays says as stock surges
feed.summary: Barclays upgraded AMD's stock to overweight from equal weight, leading to a more than 6% surge in AMD shares. Analyst Blayne Curtis believes AMD's Genoa and Bergamo platforms will enable further market-share gains against Intel. The upgrade also suggests AMD could benefit from Meta reaccelerating its spending.

feed.title: Aligning with younger employees’ values is key for Canada’s Top Employers for Young People 2023
feed.summary: Canada's Top Employers for Young People 2023 prioritize aligning with younger generations' values, offering programs in diversity, inclusion, flexibility, and career development. These employers, selected by Mediacorp Canada Inc., invest heavily in students and new graduates, providing opportunities such as internships, mentoring, and tuition subsidies. The article highlights how these organizations set standards for business by catering to the needs and values of Gen Z.

feed.title: Intel shares drop on weak quarterly results and expectations for another quarter of losses
feed.summary: Intel's shares fell after the company reported weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter results and provided a soft forecast for the first quarter of 2023. The chipmaker experienced a significant decline in revenue for the fourth consecutive quarter due to a downturn in PC chip sales and broader economic headwinds. The company also announced a net loss for Q4 and anticipates further losses in Q1, leading to concerns about its financial performance in the near future.

feed.title: The uranium bull market is just getting started
feed.summary: Frank Curzio interviews Amir Adnani, CEO of Uranium Energy Corp. (UEC), discussing why the uranium market is poised for significant growth. Adnani highlights geopolitical shifts, Japan's renewed interest in nuclear power, and the supply/demand imbalance as key drivers. He also details UEC's strategic acquisitions, particularly the Roughrider project, positioning the company as a leading unhedged uranium play.

feed.title: Intel's 'historic collapse' erases $8 billion from market value
feed.summary: Intel Corp saw its market value drop by about $8 billion after it issued dismal earnings projections and a surprise loss forecast for the first quarter, exacerbating fears about a slump in the personal-computer market and slowing data center growth. Analysts view this as a "historic collapse," with Intel losing market share to rivals like AMD due to superior chip technology and price-performance advantages. CEO Pat Gelsinger faces significant challenges in his efforts to restore Intel's dominance, with some analysts suggesting the company may need to cut its dividend due to high capital expenditures.

feed.title: AMD earnings face even more scrutiny after ‘astonishingly bad’ Intel outlook
feed.summary: AMD's upcoming earnings report is under increased scrutiny following Intel's "astonishingly bad" outlook, particularly regarding inventory and data-center issues. CEO Lisa Su had previously expressed confidence that AMD would end 2022 in a stronger position, and the market will now evaluate that claim in light of rival Intel's struggles. AMD is scheduled to report its results after market close on Tuesday.

feed.title: An open-source option is shaking up the microchip industry
feed.summary: RISC-V, an open-standard instruction set architecture developed at UC Berkeley, is rapidly gaining traction in the microchip industry. Unlike proprietary ISAs like x86 and ARM, RISC-V offers flexibility, scalability, and extensibility, allowing companies to design custom, optimized chips for various devices, from laptops to autonomous car systems. This open approach is saving tech companies time and money, and its adoption is expected to continue growing significantly in the coming years.

feed.title: ‘Everybody was wrong’: The $160B memory-chip sector is suffering one of its worst routs ever despite vows to escape the boom-and-bust cycle
feed.summary: The $160 billion memory-chip sector is currently experiencing one of its most severe and unprecedented downturns, despite earlier industry assurances of more predictable earnings. This crisis, marked by chip gluts, plunging prices, and order cuts, is severely impacting major players like SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc., destabilizing suppliers, and affecting Asian economies. The industry is responding with production cuts and investment reductions, with consolidation expected, particularly in the NAND market, as companies navigate a complex mix of economic challenges.

feed.title: W3BCLOUD SPAC: Everything you need to know about W3BCLOUD stock
feed.summary: W3BCLOUD, a technology company focused on providing infrastructure for web3 technologies, plans to go public via a SPAC merger with Social Leverage Acquisition Corp I. Despite a "crypto winter" leading to 95% of shares being redeemed by investors and several other crypto companies cancelling SPAC plans, W3BCLOUD is seeking an extension to raise the necessary $150 million for the merger. The company, a joint venture between AMD and ConsenSys, builds and manages decentralized data centers and was most recently valued at $1.25 billion (though this has been adjusted due to redemptions) and aims to expand its global network to support the growing web3 ecosystem.

feed.title: Points of light for Intel Israel
feed.summary: Intel's recent financial results were poor, and its acquisition of Tower Semiconductor faces delays due to US-China tensions. Despite these challenges, there are positive developments for Intel Israel, including the continued success of Mobileye, positive reception for the Sapphire Rapids processor, and the upcoming Lunar Lake processor and a new Kiryat Gat fab.

feed.title: LG Displays adopts its MLA technology to its new gaming monitors
feed.summary: LG Display is now incorporating its META technology, a micro-lens array (MLA), into its new gaming monitors. This technology, which increases OLED brightness by 60% and viewing angles by 30%, was initially used in LG's high-end TV panels. The company views the growing gaming market as a key area for future growth, with MLA helping to maximize light emission and enhance energy efficiency in OLED panels.

feed.title: AMD earnings: What to expect
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is reporting earnings on Tuesday, facing lowered expectations from Wall Street following Intel's recent quarterly report. Analysts anticipate AMD will issue a weak 2023 forecast, similar to Intel's, due to inventory clearance impacting gross margins and a contracting data-center market. Despite this, AMD has little room for error after its own weak outlook last quarter.

feed.title: AMD CEO promises to keep taking data-center from Intel even as cloud demand pauses following 'strong' 2022
feed.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su has pledged to continue gaining data-center market share from Intel, despite an anticipated pause in cloud demand after a robust 2022. Following Intel's worst year since the dot-com bust, AMD's stock rose as its Q1 2023 sales forecast of over $5 billion reassured Wall Street. This positive outlook comes as AMD's data-center sales grew, even with initial light demand from cloud customers.

feed.title: Why AMD is faring much better than Intel in the same tough economy
feed.summary: AMD is showing a much brighter short-term outlook compared to Intel, despite both operating in a difficult chip market. While Intel faces widespread declines, AMD's data center business is experiencing growth, aided by new chip introductions and its acquisition of Xilinx. Both companies are affected by a slumping PC market, but AMD expects to gain market share in that segment and grow its data center revenue at Intel's expense this year.

feed.title: AMD Shares Climb as Server Market Helps Sales Forecast
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) saw its shares climb after providing a better-than-expected sales forecast for the first quarter. The company's sales in the lucrative server market helped offset a significant decline in demand for PC chips. AMD expects revenue of up to $5.6 billion, surpassing analyst predictions.

feed.title: Data Center Sales Drive AMD's Q4 Resurgence
feed.summary: AMD reported better-than-expected Q4 results, driven by strong data center sales which helped offset declining demand for PC chips. The company forecasts first-quarter revenue of up to $5.6 billion and expects its data center business to continue growing, allowing it to gain market share against rivals like Intel despite an overall chip glut.

feed.title: AMD revenue beats targets, Wall St relieved after Intel's grim outlook
feed.summary: AMD announced revenue that surpassed Wall Street expectations, with its shares rising about 1.5% in after-hours trading, offering relief to investors following a grim outlook from rival Intel. Despite a challenging PC market leading to a 51% drop in client segment revenue for AMD, its Data Center segment saw a 42% increase. CEO Lisa Su expressed confidence in continued market share gains and a stronger second half of the year, contrasting with Intel's struggles in inventory correction and slumping PC business.

feed.title: AMD Ryzen 7000X3D CPUs with 3D V-Cache will start shipping later this month
feed.summary: AMD has announced the pricing and availability for its Ryzen 7000X3D series processors, featuring 3D V-Cache technology to boost performance, especially for gaming. The Ryzen 9 7950X3D and Ryzen 9 7900X3D will launch on February 28, 2023, with the Ryzen 7 7800X3D following on April 6, 2023. AMD claims its flagship chip offers a 10% improvement over Intel's high-end processors for gaming.

feed.title: Austin FC adds sponsors including Chevron, AMD ahead of 2023 season
feed.summary: Austin FC has secured new sponsors, including Chevron and AMD, as it prepares for the 2023 season. The club had a successful 2022, reaching the Western Conference finals and accumulating 107 sponsorships, making it one of Major League Soccer's most valuable teams. This article highlights the team's continued success in attracting corporate partnerships.

feed.title: Brutal Earnings From Intel Signal Changing of Guard in Chips
feed.summary: Intel's recent devastating earnings report highlights a significant shift in the semiconductor industry, with Asian giants Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) and Samsung Electronics Co. demonstrating greater resilience amidst a market downturn. While Intel and SK Hynix faced record losses and cut spending, TSMC and Samsung maintained capital expenditure plans, showcasing their emerging dominance. This signals a changing of the guard, as Intel, once an industry leader, struggles to keep pace.

feed.title: AMD Ryzen 7040 Phoenix Mobile Specs Show No Sign of PCIe 5.0
feed.summary: AMD's new Ryzen 7040 Series 'Phoenix' mobile processors, designed for ultrathin laptops, will feature PCIe 4.0 with 20 native lanes, lacking support for PCIe 5.0. Although these chips include Zen 4 CPU cores and RDNA 3 graphics, they still provide a full PCIe 4.0 x16 interface for dedicated GPUs and a PCIe 4.0 x4 link for M.2 NVMe storage. The Ryzen 7 7840H, a member of this family, is also noted for its support of both LPDDR5 and LPDDR5x RAM types and up to 256GB of memory.

feed.title: AI-designed Chips Reach Scale with First 100 Commercial Tape-outs Using Synopsys Technology
feed.summary: Synopsys announced that its AI-driven chip design system, Synopsys DSO.ai™, has achieved 100 commercial tape-outs by major semiconductor customers. Companies like STMicroelectronics and SK hynix have seen significant productivity and power, performance, and area (PPA) improvements using this technology, often leveraging cloud-based solutions like Microsoft Azure. This milestone signifies a new era for chip design, accelerating the development of advanced-node chips and optimizing design workflows with AI.

feed.title: Equinix uses two-phase cooling on live Metal cloud servers
feed.summary: Equinix has implemented two-phase liquid cooling on live production servers for its Metal infrastructure-as-a-service offering, using ZutaCore technology. This system, which involves a fluid boiling and condensing to remove heat more efficiently, has been running stably for six months in its NY5 data center. The company is testing this on standard hardware to prepare for higher thermal design power (TDP) servers and address potential future industry needs for more efficient cooling solutions, despite current PUE metrics not fully capturing its benefits.

feed.title: Qualcomm Introduces the World's First 5G NR-Light Modem-RF System to Fuel a New Wave of 5G Devices
feed.summary: Qualcomm Technologies has announced the Snapdragon X35 5G Modem-RF System, the world’s first 5G NR-Light modem-RF system, designed to bridge the gap between high-speed mobile broadband and low-bandwidth IoT devices. This new system aims to expand 5G to new use cases like smartwatches, XR glasses, and industrial IoT by offering smaller, more cost-efficient devices with longer battery life. Customer sampling is expected in the first half of 2023, with commercial devices launching by the first half of 2024.

feed.title: Fortinet Continues to Invest in Custom Chips to Power Security Offerings
feed.summary: Fortinet continues to invest in its custom ASIC chip strategy, which executives claim is a unique differentiator and a key factor in the vendor's firewall market dominance. The company designs its own Arm-based ASICs for its products to achieve better performance, efficiency, and cost savings compared to traditional CPU-based solutions. This long-term investment also provides supply chain advantages and contributes to Fortinet's leading market share in firewall appliance unit shipments.

feed.title: AMD wins nearly a third of processor market, Arm's climb slows: analyst report
feed.summary: AMD has significantly increased its market share in central processor units, capturing nearly a third of the x86 processor market from Intel, according to a recent analyst report from Mercury Research. This growth occurred during the worst PC chip market downturn since the 1980s. Meanwhile, Arm's rise in the PC market, primarily driven by Apple's Mac computers, slowed in the fourth quarter of 2022.

feed.title: Here's A Simple Way To Bet On Semiconductor Plays Like NVDA Stock
feed.summary: The semiconductor sector is experiencing a resurgence fueled by a new Nasdaq "power trend," offering investors opportunities in stocks like Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor, Advanced Micro Devices, and Broadcom. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) provides a way to capitalize on these rallies. The article highlights the SMH ETF as a simple investment vehicle for those looking to profit from the recovery and growth in semiconductor stocks.

feed.title: How enterprises can use ChatGPT and GPT-3
feed.summary: This article explores how enterprises can leverage chatbot platforms like ChatGPT and GPT-3 for various functions, from automating tasks and generating creative ideas to aiding in code development and customer support. It highlights the differences between ChatGPT and GPT-3, emphasizing GPT-3's broader capabilities and customizability for specific business needs through prompt engineering. The piece also addresses the critical risks associated with using these technologies, such as data security concerns and the potential for inaccurate or biased outputs, urging caution and the implementation of clear governance guidelines.

feed.title: Wi-SUN Alliance Certifies Silicon Labs’ Wi-SUN SoC for Smart Metering
feed.summary: Silicon Labs' ERF32FG25 Wi-SUN-enabled System-on-Chip (SoC) has received full certification from the Wi-SUN Alliance, making it available for smart city applications. This SoC addresses historical interoperability issues in the IoT and smart city industries by providing a standardized solution based on the IEEE 802.15.4g protocol. The device features a 32-bit Arm Cortex-M33 core, extensive memory, and a high-performance transceiver designed for long-range, high-throughput, and low-latency sub-GHz Wi-SUN applications.

feed.title: Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 C30 Review: Optimized For Zen 4
feed.summary: The Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 C30 memory kit is an enthusiast-grade option optimized for AMD's Ryzen 7000 processors with EXPO profiles. It offers stable performance out-of-the-box and features a clean design with customizable RGB lighting. While it performs well, especially on Intel systems, its overclocking headroom is limited due to requiring 1.4V for its advertised speeds.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
feed.summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) fell by 0.90% to $85.18 on Wednesday, despite a generally positive trading session for the stock market. The decline broke a two-day winning streak for AMD, which closed significantly below its 52-week high. Compared to competitors, AMD's performance was mixed, with NVIDIA Corp. also falling, while Intel Corp. and Applied Materials Inc. saw gains.

feed.title: The 7 Best ChatGPT Stocks to Buy Now
feed.summary: This article identifies seven top stocks poised to benefit from the rise of ChatGPT and AI, focusing on companies with established AI development rather than speculative firms. It highlights Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Alphabet (GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Salesforce (CRM) as strong investment opportunities. The author emphasizes scrutinizing a company's long-term AI commitment and business model integration to avoid hype-driven investments.

feed.title: How to Invest in the Right AI Stocks
feed.summary: The article discusses the complexities of investing in AI stocks, noting that while many major tech firms use AI, it has not yet become a significant standalone revenue driver for most. It suggests that investors should focus on "picks and shovels" companies, particularly semiconductor manufacturers like Nvidia, AMD, Intel, and ASML, which supply the essential hardware for AI development. While many companies integrate AI to enhance existing products, Morningstar analysts see direct revenue opportunities primarily in the semiconductor sector.

feed.title: How to invest in the right AI stocks
feed.summary: Investing directly in AI stocks is not straightforward, as AI's impact on company revenues is still emerging, with semiconductor companies like Nvidia being the primary direct beneficiaries. Many companies integrate AI to enhance existing products and operations rather than create new revenue streams. Investors should consider "picks and shovels" suppliers in the semiconductor industry, such as AMD and Intel, which are currently undervalued, while Nvidia is fairly valued.

feed.title: AMD Puts Hopes on Packaging, Memory on Logic, Optical Comms for Decade Ahead
feed.summary: AMD outlined its decade-long strategy for continued performance and efficiency gains in computing at ISSCC 2023. The company aims to overcome tapering process technology benefits through advanced architecture, packaging technologies like 3D CPU & GPU silicon, and tighter integration of compute and memory, including processing-in-memory solutions. Additionally, AMD plans to leverage optical communications integrated with compute dies for efficiency boosts, continuing its fierce competition in the semiconductor industry with ever faster and more efficient chips.

feed.title: Pushing the System-in-Package Concept Into the Future
feed.summary: Chipletz, a fabless substrate vendor, has selected Siemens semiconductor packaging technologies for their Smart Substrate based advanced packages. This technology aims to revolutionize semiconductor in-package functionality, bridging the gap between Moore's Law slowing and the rising demand for compute performance for critical AI workloads and high-performance computing. Chipletz's unique approach enables the integration of multiple ICs from different manufacturers within a single package, supported by Siemens' EDA tools.

feed.title: ET Global Indian award: A passion for networking helped this 'Entrepreneur at Heart' make the switch
feed.summary: Jayshree Ullal, CEO of Arista Networks, received the ET Global Indian award for her leadership in transforming Arista into a major player in the computer networking industry. Her move from Cisco to the then-startup Arista in 2008 was considered risky but led to significant growth, increasing the company's market cap from $2.5 billion to $38.52 billion. Ullal emphasizes her passion for networking and engaging with customers, advocating for skill-based valuation regardless of gender in the tech industry.

feed.title: In the Wake of Chip Shortages, TSMC Considers Investing in Germany
feed.summary: Germany, once a global economic powerhouse, has faced significant challenges due to supply chain disruptions and energy shortages, particularly an inadequate domestic semiconductor supply. To address this, Germany is actively seeking foreign investment in its semiconductor sector, with a potential investment from TSMC offering substantial economic and political gains for both parties. Such a move could bolster Germany's economy, solidify its position in Europe's semiconductor market, and provide TSMC with access to the vast European market.

feed.title: Intel cuts dividend to lowest since 2007 to save cash
feed.summary: Intel Corp has cut its quarterly dividend payout by 66% to $0.125 per share, the lowest level since 2007, to conserve cash. This decision comes as the company faces slowing demand for its chips amid a deteriorating macro-economic environment and aims to scale back major investments in manufacturing equipment. CEO Pat Gelsinger indicated the move was necessary to prioritize capital for its foundry business and achieve significant cost reductions by 2025.

feed.title: Meet the $10,000 Nvidia chip powering the race for A.I.
feed.summary: The Nvidia A100 chip, costing around $10,000, has become a critical tool in the artificial intelligence industry, powering applications like ChatGPT and Stable Diffusion. Nvidia dominates the market for GPUs used in machine learning, with the A100 serving as a "workhorse" for AI professionals and requiring substantial investment for companies to train and deploy AI models. Despite the high costs and emerging competition, Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang asserts that their GPUs offer a much more affordable solution for developing sophisticated AI models compared to traditional CPU-based data centers.

feed.title: As chatbots boom, Nvidia sales outlook beats Wall Street expectations
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp (NVDA.O) surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue expectations, driven by a surge in demand for its chips used in AI services like chatbots. The company announced a new cloud computing service to help businesses develop generative AI, positioning itself to significantly benefit from the growing AI sector despite a broader downturn in the PC market. Analysts project this boom in generative AI could add an additional $14 billion to Nvidia's revenue by 2027.

feed.title: The Future of AI Stocks: 7 Companies to Keep Your Eye On
feed.summary: This article identifies seven key AI stocks and ETFs that investors should consider due to artificial intelligence's potential to revolutionize various industries. It highlights companies like Nvidia, Microsoft, and Advanced Micro Devices for their direct involvement in AI technology, along with Innodata and C3.ai for their specific AI solutions. Additionally, it suggests AI-focused ETFs like ROBO Global Artificial Intelligence ETF and Global X Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF for diversified investment in the burgeoning AI sector.

feed.title: PWB Top Holdings List & Exposure (Invesco Large Cap Growth ETF)
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the Invesco Large Cap Growth ETF (PWB) as of May 22, 2026. Key holdings include Advanced Micro Devices, Micron Technology, and Amazon.com, with the top 25 making up over 70% of the fund. The ETF primarily invests in North American large-cap companies, with significant exposure to the Technology sector and the United States geographically.

feed.title: BFOR Top Holdings List & Exposure (Barron's 400 ETF)
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure breakdown for the Barron's 400 ETF (BFOR). It lists the top 25 stock holdings by weight, and provides a comprehensive analysis of the ETF's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures, primarily highlighting its significant concentration in the United States and the Financials and Technology sectors.

feed.title: IBLC Top Holdings List & Exposure (iShares Blockchain and Tech ETF)
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the iShares Blockchain and Tech ETF (IBLC). It lists the top 25 stock holdings, which notably include Circle Internet Group, Coinbase Global, and IREN, making up 96.80% of the fund. Additionally, the article breaks down IBLC's geographic, currency, sector, industry, and sub-industry exposures.

feed.title: Applied Materials stock rises on new chip-making system that helps lower costs
feed.summary: Applied Materials Inc. shares rose after the company unveiled a new chip-making system designed to lower manufacturing costs for semiconductors. The system, called Centura Sculpta, promises to save fabs like TSMC and Intel $50 per wafer. This innovation led to a notable outperformance of Applied Materials stock compared to its peers and the broader semiconductor market.

feed.title: Induction for Members Elected in 2020 and 2021
feed.summary: Members elected to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences in 2020 and 2021 were invited to an induction event in Cambridge, MA on September 9-10, 2022. The event included the Annual David M. Rubenstein Lecture featuring Yo-Yo Ma and remarks from notable new inductees like Lisa Su, Sanjay Gupta, and Kimberlé Crenshaw. Videos of these sessions are available.
feed.source: American Academy of Arts and Sciences
feed.source_domain: American Academy of Arts and Sciences

feed.title: AMD Unveils Its First 400G Network Performance IPSec Device at OFC 2023
feed.summary: AMD announced its first 400G Inline Internet Protocol Security (IPSec) processing capabilities, featuring the AMD Versal Premium series, at OFC 2023. This new device addresses the previous limitation of IPSec solutions to 10G-200G speeds, despite modern network capabilities up to 400G and 800G. The 400G security solution will allow businesses to operate high-speed networks without compromising security, throughput, or latency, utilizing integrated crypto engines and hardened network interface IP blocks with AMD EPYC processors.

feed.title: Sony Becomes AMD's Largest Customer on Booming PS5 Sales
feed.summary: Sony became AMD's largest customer in 2022, contributing 16% of the company's revenue, primarily due to soaring PlayStation 5 sales. AMD's gaming business unit, bolstered by PS5 and Xbox Series X|S console chip sales, was its top revenue generator. However, AMD anticipates a decline in gaming revenue for 2023 as console sales typically peak in their third year and component pricing is renegotiated.

feed.title: RYT Top Holdings List & Exposure (Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF)
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure breakdown for the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF (RYT). Key information includes the top 25 stock holdings by weight, with Intel, Advanced Micro Devices, and Micron Technology being the largest. The ETF primarily invests in the North America region, with significant exposure to the Technology sector, particularly Semiconductors and Software industries.

feed.title: Aehr Comments on Recent Tesla Statements on Silicon Carbide and Reiterates its Annual Guidance
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems clarified recent statements made by Tesla regarding a 75% reduction in silicon carbide for new drive units, asserting that this reduction applies only to a new, lower-cost platform still under development and will not significantly impact the overall silicon carbide wafer market. Aehr believes the shift to larger, more integrated die in custom modules for new platforms will increase the need for their wafer-level test and burn-in solutions, affirming their fiscal year 2023 revenue guidance of $60 million to $70 million.

feed.title: Microchip Technology, microchip manufacturer with capital of $50 billion, coming to Armenia
feed.summary: American microchip manufacturer Microchip Technology, with a market capital of $50 billion, is expanding its operations into Armenia. This follows a two-year collaborative agreement with Instigate company, which culminated in Microchip Technology acquiring Instigate Semiconductor. The move is part of a larger trend of major tech and energy companies, including NVIDIA, AMD, and Shell, establishing a presence in Armenia, aiming to boost the country's productivity, innovation, and expertise in high-tech fields.

feed.title: RANOVUS demonstrates industry’s lowest power consumption 800Gbps Ethernet interoperable link to scale AMD adaptive SoCs for AI/ML
feed.summary: RANOVUS has demonstrated an 800Gbps Ethernet interoperable link with the industry's lowest power consumption, designed to scale AMD adaptive SoCs for AI/ML applications. This demonstration at OFC 2023 highlights the versatility of RANOVUS's Odin® optical interconnect, which offers 5pJ/bit energy efficiency for co-packaged, near-packaged, and pluggable optical modules. The technology aims to significantly reduce power consumption and optimize density and cost for hyperscale data centers facing growing AI/ML workloads.

feed.title: Argentina’s copper rush lures top power generator Central Puerto
feed.summary: Central Puerto SA, one of Argentina's largest power producers, is looking to diversify into the nation's burgeoning copper sector. The company aims to acquire non-operating stakes in copper projects, offering its expertise to international investors in navigating Argentina's complex regulatory environment. This move is part of a broader strategy to invest in export-oriented sectors like mining and forestry, which provide revenue in US dollars, contrasting with its domestic power sales in the devaluing Argentine peso.

feed.title: Why Hidden Artificial Intelligence Features Make Such an Impact in Education
feed.summary: This article discusses how hidden Artificial Intelligence features, particularly those developed by Qualcomm Technologies, Inc., are significantly impacting education. It highlights Qualcomm's role in providing connectivity, efficient processing, and AI at the edge, emphasizing the benefits of extended battery life in devices and advanced AI functionalities like noise cancellation for educators. The piece also underlines the importance of addressing the digital divide and encourages educators to embrace AI tools to enhance learning experiences and develop higher critical thinking skills in students.

feed.title: Exclusive: Nvidia's plans for sales to Huawei imperiled if U.S. tightens Huawei curbs-draft
feed.summary: Nvidia's plans to sell technology to Huawei could be thwarted if the U.S. government implements stricter restrictions on shipments to the blacklisted Chinese company, according to a draft government contractor report. The Biden administration is currently evaluating the economic impact of potential new rules on U.S. companies before proceeding. The report indicates a "high economic impact" on Nvidia and a "moderate economic impact" on Qualcomm if the proposed policy changes are enacted.

feed.title: Supermicro Expands Storage Solutions Portfolio for Intensive I/O Workloads with All-Flash Servers Utilizing EDSFF E3.S and E1.S Storage Drives
TSMC customer billed NT$529bn
feed.summary: TSMC's largest customer, widely believed to be Apple Inc., generated NT$529.65 billion (US$17.32 billion) in revenue for the company last year, marking the first time a single customer contributed over NT$500 billion. Despite this record revenue, the customer's share of TSMC's total sales decreased from 26% in 2021 to 23% in 2022. This shift is attributed to significant growth in TSMC's automotive chip sales, which outperformed those for high-performance computing and smartphones.

feed.title: Smaller Oregon companies looking for semiconductor funding
feed.summary: Oregon lawmakers are preparing to allocate $200 million to attract federal semiconductor funding by supporting local companies and universities. While much attention has been on large fabrication plants, a network of smaller firms like Inpria and HP Inc. in Corvallis also contribute significantly to chip manufacturing. State Representative Janelle Bynum emphasized the need for a diverse workforce and better return on investment in the semiconductor industry, advocating for varied educational paths from apprenticeships to advanced degrees.

feed.title: ASML, China customers haunted by uncertainty on new Dutch chip export rules
feed.summary: The Dutch government's newly announced restrictions on chip-technology exports to China have left ASML Holding NV and its Chinese customers uncertain about crucial details, including whether ASML can service existing chip-printing machines in China. While potentially aligning with U.S. goals, the lack of clarity on servicing limits and affected machine models poses a significant challenge for ASML's outlook and its substantial sales and service agreements in the Chinese market. The situation could lead Chinese chipmakers to focus on older technology, shifting ASML's demand to other regions.

feed.title: Emerging Leaders 2023: Ankur Prakash
feed.summary: Ankur Prakash, a partner at Liberty Global Ventures, has been recognized as one of the top 50 Emerging Leaders in corporate venturing for 2023. With over a decade at Liberty Global, he has significantly impacted various tech sectors through strategic investments like Arrcus, Aviatrix, and Lacework. Prakash, an entrepreneur-first investor, focuses on helping founders disrupt large markets and build exceptional companies, leveraging Liberty Global Ventures' 15 years of investment history and strong industry network in Europe.

feed.title: AMD Shoots Down EPYC Genoa Memory Bug Claims, Says Update On Track
feed.summary: AMD has refuted recent claims of a memory bug affecting its EPYC Genoa processors that would necessitate a costly redesign to support 2DPC memory configurations. The company stated that all shipped fourth-gen EPYC processors fully support 2DPC and that BIOS updates have already been issued to OEM partners to enable this functionality by the end of Q1 2023. This clarifies previous vague statements and assures that no respin is required for the anticipated memory capacity boost.
